issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: The paragraph text uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) which is #aaaaaa against a white background (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 604: .section-header p { font-size: var(--font-size-lg); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); margin-top: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px without a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus ring entirely for keyboard users, making it impossible to see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 392: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on footer nav a
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links have a min-height of 24px, which meets the absolute minimum but falls below the 44px recommended target size. Without at least 8px of vertical padding to extend the interactive area, users may have difficulty activating these links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3119: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 44px; padding-block: 10px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This selector inherits outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule and only sets outline-offset: -3px. Without an outline-width or visible box-shadow/border, no focus indicator is rendered.
issuesFound.fix: Line 415: .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which makes the content visible in the layout and does not hide it from screen readers. This is the opposite of the intended behavior; content intended for screen readers will be visible to all users, and the class fails to provide a screen-reader-only utility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 426: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button includes transform in its transition property (transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast), transform var(--transition-fast)). There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable or override this transform transition for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 665: (add after line 700) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox has an explicit width and height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. This makes it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate the control.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The `:focus-visible` rule sets `outline: none` and only an `outline-offset`, providing no visible focus indicator for keyboard users, which blocks access to focus cues.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible

issuesFound.fix: Line 629: .btn { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a selector sets min-height: 28px. Dropdown menu links are often small, but 28px is below the recommended 44px touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 776: [id$="-submenu"] li a { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement. This affects the mobile navigation toggle button, making it difficult for keyboard users to identify when it is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 723: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px. This is below the 24px absolute minimum requirement for touch targets, making icon buttons very difficult to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 695: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Keyboard users navigating dropdown menus will not see which link is currently focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 766: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input sets height: 28px. Form inputs should be at least 44px high to ensure they are easy to tap and activate the on-screen keyboard comfortably.
issuesFound.fix: Line 794: #search-input { height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account nav links
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement. Keyboard users navigating the account menu will not see the focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 848: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm selector sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. This makes the button difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 876: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] selector sets width: 20px and height: 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1445: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector includes transform in its transition property, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty and does not disable this animation, potentially causing issues for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 541-545: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector includes transform in its transition, but the corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty and does not override the animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1406-1410: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words and can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 376: body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for secondary text
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector references var(--colour-text-secondary) which is set to #aaaaaa on background #ffffff, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none with only an outline-offset and no visible replacement such as box-shadow, border, or a non-zero outline. Keyboard users navigating with Tab will not see any focus indicator on focused elements.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px but provides no visible alternative focus indicator such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users cannot see when buttons receive focus.

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which makes the content visible in the layout and does not hide it from sighted users. It fails to position content off-screen or clip it, meaning screen-reader-only text is displayed to all users or may not be announced correctly depending on browser.
issuesFound.fix: Line 290-301: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (line 419) and applies transform: translateY(1px) on :active (line 427). There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this spatial movement for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 956 (addition): .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } (remove transform transition) or add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator in high-contrast mode. Since box-shadow is suppressed in forced-colors mode, the outline property is essential for focus visibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 350: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only adjusts outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for keyboard users. This removes the default browser focus ring without providing a replacement such as a box-shadow or border, making it impossible for keyboard-only users to see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 335: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate these small buttons on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 605: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have min-height: 24px with no vertical padding compensation, keeping them at the minimum 24px size without reaching the recommended 44px. This may cause difficulty for users with limited precision when tapping footer links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1706: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2) 0; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule removes the default outline without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users cannot see when buttons receive focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 537: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class incorrectly uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which makes the content visible on screen rather than hiding it. Additionally, it lacks the clip or clip-path properties needed to hide content visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This breaks skip links and screen-reader-only labels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 371: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width and height of 28px with zero padding, placing it in the 24–43px range without sufficient padding to reach the recommended 44px touch target. Users may accidentally miss the button when tapping.
issuesFound.fix: Line 720: #site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 28px; height: 28px; padding: var(--space-2); background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: Icon-only buttons have min-width and min-height set to 20px, falling below the 24px minimum touch target size. This makes them difficult for users with tremors or limited dexterity to tap accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 617: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card uses transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01) on hover, creating a combined vertical and scaling animation. This motion effect is not wrapped in a prefers-reduced-motion media query, failing to respect user motion preferences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1456: .pricing-card--featured:hover { box-shadow: var(--shadow-2xl); } and add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: Checkboxes have width and height set to 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size. This makes them difficult to select accurately, especially for users with motor disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1677: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transition: background-color ..., transform var(--transition-fast)), causing buttons to animate spatial movement when states change. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this transform transition for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 539: .btn { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: var(--space-2); min-height: 32px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-5); font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-semibold); line-height: var(--line-height-tight); text-align: center; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap; cursor: pointer; user-select: none; border: 2px solid transparent; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); vertical-align: middle; position: relative; isolation: isolate; } and add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. In forced-colors mode (Windows High Contrast), box-shadow is suppressed, making outline the only reliable focus indicator. Removing it entirely creates a critical barrier for high-contrast users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global :focus-visible selector
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px without a visible outline style, box-shadow, or border. This removes the focus ring entirely for keyboard users, making it impossible to see which element has focus. The outline-offset property has no visible effect when the outline width is zero.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on footer links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a selector sets min-height: 24px with no padding compensation specified in the rule. While this meets the absolute minimum of 24px, it falls short of the 44px recommended target size and may be difficult for some users to activate reliably.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset
issuesFound.explanation: The .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible selector only adjusts outline-offset to -3px but does not restore a visible outline width or provide an alternative such as box-shadow. Since the global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none, this element has no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which makes the content visible in the layout rather than hiding it visually. This breaks the intended functionality of providing content only to screen readers and will cause visual layout issues while failing to properly hide content.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with padding: 0, resulting in a touch target of only 28×28 pixels. This is below the 44px recommended size and the 24px absolute minimum, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on icon buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Icon-only buttons are particularly critical for touch accessibility and must meet minimum size requirements.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand
issuesFound.explanation: The .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible selector only sets outline-color but does not set outline-width or outline-style, resulting in no visible focus ring. The inherited outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule remains in effect.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a selector uses color: var(--colour-neutral-400) which is #94a3b8 against a footer background of var(--colour-neutral-900) which is #555555, providing approximately 2.83:1 contrast and failing WCAG AA.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and removes the focus indicator without replacement. While the :focus state has a box-shadow, the :focus-visible override nullifies it, leaving keyboard users without a visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px and border-radius, but lacks a visible outline, box-shadow, or border to indicate focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, providing no visible focus ring or alternative indicator for this interactive button.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without providing a replacement visual indicator such as box-shadow or border. Interactive buttons will show no focus ring to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on feature card links
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, leaving no visible focus indicator for these card links.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on FAQ buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: -3px, providing no visible focus ring for these disclosure buttons.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on checkbox inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, removing the focus indicator from checkbox controls without replacement.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, leaving no visible focus indicator for footer links.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] has width: 20px, height: 20px, and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Custom checkboxes must meet minimum size requirements or have sufficient padding to achieve 24×24px.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on social links
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, providing no visible focus ring for social media links.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer address
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-brand address selector uses color: var(--colour-neutral-500) which is #64748b against #555555, providing approximately 1.93:1 contrast. This is well below the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1.

issuesFound.title: :focus-visible removes focus indicator
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing any visible replacement (no box‑shadow, border, or non‑zero outline). Keyboard users lose the focus ring on all focusable elements.

issuesFound.title: Negative word‑spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body rule sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses word gaps and harms readability for all users.

issuesFound.title: :focus:not(:focus-visible) removes focus indicator
issuesFound.explanation: This rule also sets outline: none and offers no visual fallback, so mouse‑only interactions also lose any focus styling.

issuesFound.title: .hero-image img motion animation lacks reduced‑motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image transitions transform and box‑shadow on hover, but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override disables this motion, violating WCAG 2.3.3.

issuesFound.title: .btn-sm touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant has min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum of 44 px, making it hard to tap on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: Global a { text-decoration: none; } removes the default underline from all links, and no non‑colour visual cue (border‑bottom, font‑weight, background) is supplied, making links indistinguishable from surrounding text.

issuesFound.title: .nav-toggle:focus-visible removes focus indicator
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes its outline with outline: none and provides no alternative visual cue.

issuesFound.title: .btn:focus-visible removes focus indicator
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons lose their focus ring because the selector only sets outline: none and outline-offset without a visible replacement.

issuesFound.title: .footer-brand a:focus-visible removes focus indicator

issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, which renders no visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot identify focused elements.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector includes transform in its transition property (transition: background-color ..., transform var(--transition-fast)), which can cause motion animation. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable or override this transform transition for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector has transition: transform var(--transition-slower) and transform: perspective(...) on hover. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 724 is empty and does not override these animations, potentially causing vestibular issues for sensitive users.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which is a negative value that reduces space between words. This can significantly impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and may cause words to overlap in some font rendering scenarios.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on secondary text
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p selector uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) which is #aaaaaa against a white background (#ffffff). This provides a contrast ratio of approximately 2.85:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on hero note text

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on logo bar labels
issuesFound.explanation: The .logo-bar-label selector uses color: var(--colour-neutral-400) which is #94a3b8 against var(--colour-neutral-50) which is #f8fafc. This combination provides approximately 2.85:1 contrast, failing the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on stat labels
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label selector uses color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) against a gradient background of #5588bb to #bb88ee. The effective contrast is well below 4.5:1, making this text difficult to read for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid omits aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. This prevents screen readers from identifying the field as having a validation error, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1296: Insert the following line immediately after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`:
field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() omits aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open function reveals the mobile navigation drawer by adding CSS classes and manages body scroll lock, but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves screen reader users unaware that the menu has opened. The close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating asymmetrical state management that violates WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1764: Insert the following line immediately after `addClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);`:
setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu lacks live region announcements for open/close
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels without announcing state changes to screen readers. Users navigating with keyboard or assistive technologies receive no feedback when a menu appears or disappears, creating a cognitive barrier.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance omits aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened, the _openInstance function removes the hidden attribute from the panel and adds CSS classes, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded state of the disclosure widget.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1893: Insert the following line immediately after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`:
setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open omits aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open function manages panel visibility and CSS classes but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This prevents screen readers from informing users that the answer panel is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3424: Insert the following line immediately after `addClass(item.el, CSS.open);`:
setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria-invalid on error (newsletter email)
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form’s email input is validated via `inlineValidator`, which marks the field as invalid visually but does not set `aria-invalid="true"`, leaving the error invisible to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Modify `srUtils.markInvalid` (or the inline validator) to also call `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` when an error is present.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` function toggles the navigation drawer but never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of the `open` function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The `openInstance` function reveals a dropdown panel but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the associated trigger button, breaking the ARIA state relationship.
issuesFound.fix: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening the dropdown.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing initial aria-expanded on load
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ triggers are parsed without an initial `aria-expanded` attribute, causing an undefined state for screen readers before any interaction.
issuesFound.fix: In the FAQ parsing step, set `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` for each trigger.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. The markValid function correctly removes aria-invalid, creating an asymmetry where invalid fields are not programmatically identified as invalid.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1195: function markInvalid(field, errorEl) {
  Line 1197: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open function adds the open class to the drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the _trigger button. The close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating a state mismatch where screen readers announce the menu as closed even when it is open.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1330: function open(announceToSR) {
  Line 1335: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The close function emits a custom event but does not call liveRegion.announce to inform screen reader users that the navigation menu has closed. The open function correctly announces the state change, leading to inconsistent feedback.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1350: function close(announceToSR) {
  Line 1375: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function removes the hidden attribute from the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the inst.trigger button. The paired _closeInstance function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", causing screen readers to misidentify the menu state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1475: function _openInstance(inst) {
  Line 1478: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open function sets the item state to true and removes the hidden attribute from the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the item.trigger button. The close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", resulting in incorrect state reporting to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3135: function open(item, announceToSR) {
  Line 3140: setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open omits aria-expanded update on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The search input implements the combobox pattern with aria-expanded, but the _open function only sets internal state without updating the aria-expanded attribute to "true". This leaves assistive technologies unaware that the suggestion listbox is visible.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2674: Insert the following line immediately after `_searchState.open = true;`:
setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons misuse aria-selected instead of aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/quarterly/yearly) use aria-selected to indicate the active state. These are button elements acting as toggle buttons, not tab elements. Toggle buttons must use aria-pressed to communicate their state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3076: Replace the line `setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));` with:
setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer omits aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is hidden via CSS class removal, but aria-hidden is not set to "true" when closed. If the CSS uses off-screen positioning (transform, absolute positioning) rather than display:none, the drawer remains in the accessibility tree, allowing screen reader users to navigate to invisible content.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle button missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle button (data-billing-toggle) is initialized without ensuring aria-expanded reflects the initial state. If the controlled pricing content is collapsed on load, the trigger must have aria-expanded="false" set so screen readers understand the initial state.

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The period selection buttons are initialized without setting aria-pressed to reflect the default selected billing period. This leaves screen readers unaware of which period is active when the page first loads.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not clear aria-invalid on success (newsletter email)
issuesFound.explanation: When the newsletter email field becomes valid, the validator removes the error message but does not remove `aria-invalid`, so the field remains incorrectly marked as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure `srUtils.markValid` also removes the `aria-invalid` attribute (`removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');`).

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria-invalid on error (contact form – name)
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form’s “Full name” field is validated but the error handling does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input.
issuesFound.fix: Update the validation routine to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field when validation fails.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria-invalid on error (contact form – email)
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form’s email field lacks `aria-invalid="true"` when an invalid email is entered.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` in the error handling path.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria-invalid on error (contact form – subject)
issuesFound.explanation: The “Subject” field’s validation does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on error, preventing screen‑reader users from detecting the problem.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure `aria-invalid` is set to true when the subject fails validation.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria-invalid on error (contact form – message)
issuesFound.explanation: The “Message” textarea’s validation does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on error, leaving the error invisible to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` when the message fails validation.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function changes visible content by revealing the dropdown panel but does not write to an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not notified that the menu has opened, unlike other components in this file that use liveRegion.announce.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1475: function _openInstance(inst) {
  Line 1485: liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function hides the dropdown panel but does not call liveRegion.announce. This omits feedback for screen reader users when the menu closes, creating an inconsistent experience compared to the open action.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1490: function _closeInstance(inst, returnFocus) {
  Line 1505: liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The close function collapses the answer panel and emits a custom event but does not call liveRegion.announce. Screen reader users are not explicitly told the answer has collapsed, whereas the open function provides this announcement.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3155: function close(item, announceToSR) {
  Line 3170: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed || 'Answer collapsed');

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader._onScroll function does not announce visibility state change
issuesFound.explanation: The _onScroll function toggles the header visibility (hide/show) based on scroll direction and emits custom events, but does not announce these changes to an aria-live region. Screen reader users are unaware when the navigation header disappears or reappears.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1630: function _onScroll() {
  Line 1635: liveRegion.announce(_hidden ? 'Header hidden' : 'Header shown');

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._showBanner function does not announce banner display
issuesFound.explanation: The _showBanner function renders and displays the cookie consent dialog but does not call liveRegion.announce. Screen reader users may not be immediately aware that a modal dialog requiring interaction has appeared on the page.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3590: function _showBanner() {
  Line 3625: liveRegion.announce('Cookie consent banner displayed');

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._hideBanner function does not announce banner dismissal
issuesFound.explanation: The _hideBanner function removes the cookie consent dialog from the DOM but does not call liveRegion.announce. Screen reader users are not notified that the banner has been dismissed, leaving them uncertain if the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3575: function _hideBanner() {
  Line 3585: liveRegion.announce('Cookie consent banner dismissed');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open function adds CSS classes to display the mobile navigation drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users cannot perceive that the menu has expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2404: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function closes the mobile navigation and updates ARIA attributes, but does not announce the state change to screen readers, leaving users unaware that the menu has closed.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened, the function removes the hidden attribute from the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, leaving the accessibility tree out of sync with the visual state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2548: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown menu reveals new content but provides no announcement to screen reader users, who may not realize the menu has appeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2548: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` immediately after the emit call in the _openInstance function.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle element is given role="switch" and keyboard support, but no accessible name is ensured. Without an aria-label, screen reader users cannot identify what the switch controls.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open function animates the answer panel into view and updates internal state, but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4074: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is closed, the content is hidden from view but no announcement is made to assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2565: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` immediately after the emit call in the _closeInstance function.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open handler does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The function _openInstance adds the “open” CSS class to the panel but never updates the trigger button’s aria‑expanded state, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the menu is expanded.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion close function does not set aria‑expanded to false on the trigger button (already present) – but the panel remains focusable after closure, violating focus management.
issuesFound.explanation: close(item) sets aria‑expanded to false but does not move focus away from the trigger, leaving keyboard users stranded on a hidden element.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close handler does not remove aria‑hidden from panel when hidden via CSS class only
issuesFound.explanation: _closeInstance sets aria‑expanded to false and removes the “open” class, but the panel is hidden only by CSS (no hidden attribute). Because the element remains in the accessibility tree, screen‑reader users may still encounter it.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open handler does not announce the state change via a live region (ARIA‑live)
issuesFound.explanation: Users of assistive technology receive no audible cue that the menu has opened.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle switch (_toggleEl) uses aria‑checked but does not set role="switch" or aria‑label for screen‑reader identification
issuesFound.explanation: Assistive technologies may not recognise the element as a switch, reducing discoverability.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close function does not set aria‑hidden on the drawer when it is hidden via CSS class only
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer is hidden by removing the “is‑open” class, but the element remains in the accessibility tree, potentially exposing hidden navigation links to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close function does not announce the closed state via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: No audible cue that the menu has collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
After setting aria‑expanded false, add:  
```javascript
liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || `${inst.trigger.textContent.trim()} menu closed`);
```

issuesFound.fix: Line 1724: Insert `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` as the first line inside the markInvalid function body.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Collapsing an FAQ answer hides content from view without notifying screen reader users, which can be disorienting if they did not trigger the close action themselves.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function does not remove aria‑hidden from the drawer when it becomes visible
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer is shown by adding the “is‑open” class, but aria‑hidden remains (if it was set elsewhere), causing the drawer to be announced as hidden even when visible.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function links error messages via aria-describedby but omits setting aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1502: Insert `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after the null checks.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open function adds CSS classes to show the navigation drawer but never updates the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". Screen readers will not announce the expanded state, leaving users unaware the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1856: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the `_open = true;` assignment.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu closes (via Escape key or outside click), no announcement is made to screen readers. Users may not realize the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1908: Insert `if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); }` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is opened, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not set to "true", violating the disclosure pattern. The attribute is only set to "false" in _closeInstance.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2002: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing dropdown menus does not trigger a live region announcement. Users navigating with screen readers receive no feedback when menu state changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2006 (in _openInstance): Insert `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` after the panel is shown. Line 2032 (in _closeInstance): Insert `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function animates the FAQ panel open and updates state, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function sets it to "false", creating an asymmetry.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3553: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: Inline form validator does not set `aria‑invalid="true"` on fields that fail validation (newsletter email)
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate` calls `srUtils.markInvalid`, which only adds an `aria-describedby` reference but never sets `aria‑invalid`. The email input in the newsletter form therefore lacks the required ARIA error state.
issuesFound.fix:
Modify `srUtils.markInvalid` (or the validator) to include `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` when an error is present.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation “open” function does not set `aria‑expanded="true"` on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` function adds the open CSS class to the drawer and announces the change, but it never updates the trigger’s `aria‑expanded` attribute to `"true"`. Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know that the navigation menu is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of `mobileNav.open()` (after the early‑return check).  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion “open” function does not set `aria‑expanded="true"` on the question button
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.open(item)` expands the answer panel and announces the change, but it never updates the trigger’s `aria‑expanded` attribute to `"true"`. Assistive technologies cannot detect that the FAQ item is open.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` in the `open()` function.  

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open handler does not set `aria‑expanded="true"` on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu._openInstance()`, the panel is shown and the `open` flag is set, but the trigger’s `aria‑expanded` attribute is never updated to `"true"`. This leaves the accessibility state out of sync with the visual state.
issuesFound.fix:
Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` right after `inst.open = true;` in `_openInstance()`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Inline form validator does not set `aria‑invalid="true"` on contact‑form “Message” textarea errors
issuesFound.explanation: Validation failures for the message textarea are not announced via `aria‑invalid`.
issuesFound.fix:
Update the validator to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the textarea when the message is too short or too long.  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger buttons lack an initial `aria‑expanded="false"` attribute on page load
issuesFound.explanation: During parsing (`faqRegistry.parse`), the trigger’s `aria-controls` is set, but the initial `aria‑expanded` state is never defined. When the page loads the FAQ items are collapsed, but the ARIA state does not reflect that, violating the requirement that toggle widgets must have an accurate ARIA state on load.
issuesFound.fix:
In `faqRegistry.parse`, after `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId);` add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.  

---

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The open function never activates the focus trap (_trap is declared but never assigned or activated). Keyboard users can tab outside the open mobile menu into the background content.

issuesFound.title: Inline form validator does not clear `aria‑invalid` on fields that become valid (newsletter email)
issuesFound.explanation: When a previously invalid field becomes valid, `srUtils.markValid` removes the error description but does **not** remove or set `aria‑invalid="false"`. The field remains marked as invalid to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
In `srUtils.markValid`, after removing the error description, add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` (or set it to `"false"`).

issuesFound.title: Inline form validator does not set `aria‑invalid="true"` on contact‑form “Email address” field errors
issuesFound.explanation: The email input’s validation error is not reflected via `aria‑invalid`, breaking the required ARIA error signalling.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the validator sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the email field when the email pattern fails.

issuesFound.title: Inline form validator does not clear `aria‑invalid` on contact‑form “Email address” field after correction
issuesFound.explanation: The attribute remains after the user fixes the email, leaving the field incorrectly flagged as invalid.
issuesFound.fix:
Remove or set `aria-invalid="false"` when the email passes validation.

issuesFound.title: Inline form validator does not clear `aria‑invalid` on contact‑form “Message” textarea after correction
issuesFound.explanation: The `aria-invalid` attribute remains after the user corrects the message length.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the validator removes the attribute (or sets it to `"false"`) when the textarea becomes valid.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions toggle CSS classes on the navigation drawer and body, but never call setAttribute('aria-expanded', ...) or assign .ariaExpanded on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen readers cannot announce the expanded/collapsed state of the mobile menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1350: Add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); Line 1368: Add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close does not announce state via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions in dropdownMenu change the visible state of dropdown panels but do not write to an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not informed when a dropdown menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1277: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened'); Line 1292: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not initialise aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function locates the trigger button (_trigger) but does not set aria-expanded="false" during initialisation. Since the menu is closed by default, the trigger must communicate this state to assistive technologies immediately on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1405: Add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function toggles the mobile drawer but never sets aria‑expanded on the hamburger <button> that controls it, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the drawer’s state.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') when opening and setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') when closing.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer is not announced as visible to AT when opened
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.open() does not remove aria‑hidden from the drawer, so even though the drawer becomes visible, AT still treats it as hidden.
issuesFound.fix: After adding the open class, call removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden').

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function does not set aria‑expanded on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: _openInstance() adds the “open” class to the panel but never updates aria‑expanded on the <button> that toggles the dropdown, breaking the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') when opening.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have an aria-controls attribute pointing to the drawer element (_drawer). Screen reader users cannot identify which element is controlled by the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1406: Add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id || srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap declared but never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The _trap variable is declared (line 1324) and referenced in open() (line 1341) and close() (line 1367), but createFocusTrap() is never called to initialise it. Consequently, keyboard focus is not trapped within the mobile menu when open, and focus is not returned to the trigger button on close, violating WCAG 2.4.3 and 2.4.7.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1347: Add _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates function toggles CSS classes to indicate the active filter tab, but does not set the aria-pressed attribute on the buttons. Filter tabs function as toggle buttons (showing the active state) and require aria-pressed to communicate their state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2299: Change toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); to toggleAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, 'true', 'false', isActive);

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The _renderResult function in inlineValidator marks fields as invalid by adding CSS classes and updating aria-describedby via srUtils.markInvalid, but never calls setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true') on the invalid field. Screen readers cannot identify the invalid state of the input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3047: Add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true'); inside the else block (invalid branch) before or after srUtils.markInvalid call.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the _renderResult function removes error classes and calls srUtils.markValid, but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false". The field remains marked as invalid to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3045: Add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false'); or removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge update not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The _updateCountBadge function updates the textContent and aria-label of the count badge, but because the element is not a live region, the change is not announced. Dynamic content updates must be announced via an aria-live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2328: Add liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`); or ensure the _countBadge element has aria-live="polite".

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-haspopup attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button controls a menu (the navigation drawer) but does not have aria-haspopup="menu" or "dialog". Screen reader users cannot identify that the button opens a menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1407: Add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.HASPOPUP, 'menu');

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer is not hidden from AT when closed
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close() removes CSS classes and hides the drawer visually but never adds aria‑hidden="true" to the drawer element, so the hidden panel remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.close() call setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and in mobileNav.open() call removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden').

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function does not remove aria‑hidden from the panel
issuesFound.explanation: The panel’s hidden attribute is removed, but aria‑hidden remains, so AT still treats the panel as hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Call removeAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden') when opening.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close function does not set aria‑expanded to false on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: _closeInstance() removes the open class but never updates aria‑expanded, leaving the trigger’s state stale for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') when closing.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close function does not set aria‑hidden on the panel
issuesFound.explanation: The panel is hidden visually but aria‑hidden is not set, so the panel stays in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Call setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true') when closing.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded to true on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() expands the answer panel but never updates aria‑expanded on the associated <button>, so screen‑reader users cannot tell whether the item is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') when opening.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not remove aria‑hidden from the answer panel
issuesFound.explanation: The panel’s hidden attribute is removed, but aria‑hidden remains, leaving the content hidden from AT.
issuesFound.fix: Call removeAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden') when opening.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion close function does not set aria‑hidden to true on the answer panel (only adds hidden attribute)
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.close() adds the hidden attribute but does not set aria‑hidden, so the panel may still be exposed to AT.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true') when closing.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not setting aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the code adds error classes and updates aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid input field. Screen readers cannot identify which fields have errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4140: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);` Line 4132: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` after `addClass(field, CSS.fieldValid);` in the valid branch.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle button missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button controls the drawer element but does not have an aria-controls attribute pointing to the drawer's ID. Screen reader users cannot identify which element is controlled by the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2424: After ensuring the drawer has an ID (using `srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav')`), add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When users apply filters, the product grid updates and the count badge changes, but there is no aria-live region announcement to inform screen reader users how many results are showing or that the filter has been applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3336: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : \`Showing \${totalMatches} products\`);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus changes the visible content significantly, but there is no announcement via the live region to inform screen reader users that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2490: After `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` Line 2507: After `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: pricing billing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing, the prices update visually, but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users may not realize the prices have changed or what the new amounts are.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3580: After `planDataParser.getAll().forEach(plan => _updatePlanCard(plan, period));`, add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons (`[data-filter-value]`) toggle the visibility of the product grid but do not have aria-controls attributes pointing to the grid container. Screen reader users cannot identify which element is controlled by the tabs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3412: After `_gridEl` is assigned, ensure it has an ID (`srUtils.ensureId(_gridEl, 'product-grid')`), then in `_syncTabAriaStates` or during tab initialization, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-controls', _gridEl.id);` for each tab button.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding and collapsing FAQ answers changes visible content, but the state change is not announced to screen reader users. Users may not realize the answer has expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3860: After `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:open', { item });`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question));` Line 3890: After `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:close', { item });`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question));`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not clearing aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the code removes error styling and aria-describedby references but does not explicitly set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute. Screen readers may still report the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4132: Add `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` after `srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: Inline form validator does not set aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate marks fields as invalid visually but never adds aria‑invalid="true" to the input element, so screen‑reader users are not informed of validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: In _renderResult when result.valid is false add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); and when valid remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.toggle function (and its open/close helpers) change the visibility of the navigation drawer but never set aria‑expanded on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: In open() add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') and in close() add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: Search result count not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: After applying a filter in productFilter.applyFilters the visible product count is updated in the badge (`_countBadge`) but no aria‑live region is used, so users of assistive technology are not informed of the new count.
issuesFound.fix: Add a hidden <div role="status" aria-live="polite"> and update its text with the count each time applyFilters runs.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions toggle the visibility of dropdown panels but never set aria‑expanded on the associated trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: In _ openInstance() add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); in _closeInstance() add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) acts as a binary toggle (open/close) but never receives an aria‑pressed state, violating ARIA toggle button pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In open() add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true') and in close() add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'false').

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter tab buttons lack aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tabs (`[data-filter-value]` buttons) indicate the active category via CSS classes only; they do not expose their pressed state via aria‑pressed, which is required for toggle‑style button groups.
issuesFound.fix: In _productFilter._syncTabAriaStates add setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive)) for each tab button.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons lack aria‑expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The period toggle buttons (`[data-period]`) act as a mutually exclusive set but do not expose their selected state via aria‑expanded or aria‑pressed, making it unclear which billing period is active.
issuesFound.fix: In _pricing._syncToggleState add setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive)) or setAttr(btn, 'aria-expanded', String(isActive)).

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid function missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() utility function associates an error message via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field. This is a critical failure for form validation accessibility as screen readers rely on aria-invalid to announce that a field contains an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 963: function markInvalid(field, errorEl) { ... field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); addDescribedBy(field, errorEl); }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger aria-expanded not updated in toggle functions
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions in the mobileNav module toggle the navigation drawer visibility but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the _trigger element. Screen reader users will not be informed whether the menu is currently open or closed when interacting with the toggle button.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1683: function open(announceToSR) { ... setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); ... }
  Line 1712: function close(announceToSR) { ... setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); ... }

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the faqAccordion module expands the answer panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the item.trigger. The close() function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state where screen readers are not notified when an item opens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2553: function open(item, announceToSR) { ... setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); ... }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger aria-expanded not initialised at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The init() function in the mobileNav module does not set the initial aria-expanded state on the _trigger element. If the menu is closed by default, the trigger should have aria-expanded="false" set during initialization to reflect the correct starting state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1769: function init() { ... if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); ... }

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters updates content without live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters() function updates the visible product grid and count badge dynamically but does not announce these changes to an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not informed that the product list has been filtered or that the result count has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2253: function applyFilters() { ... liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`); ... }

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod updates prices without live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The setPeriod() function updates all plan card prices dynamically when the billing period changes but does not announce this change via a live region. Users relying on screen readers are not notified that the prices on the page have been updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2903: function setPeriod(period, animate) { ... liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`); ... }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger aria-expanded not updated in toggle functions
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions in the dropdownMenu module show and hide the panel but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the inst.trigger element. This prevents assistive technologies from tracking the current state of the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1862: function _openInstance(inst) { ... setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); ... }
  Line 1880: function _closeInstance(inst, returnFocus) { ... setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); ... }

issuesFound.title: srUtils markValid function missing aria-invalid removal
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() utility function clears the error message association but does not remove or set aria-invalid="false" on the field. The field may remain in an invalid state in the accessibility tree even after the user has corrected the error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 978: function markValid(field, errorEl) { ... field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false'); ... }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing focus management on open
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, focus remains on the trigger button instead of moving to the drawer or its first focusable element. Keyboard users may not realize the menu is open or may have difficulty accessing it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2350: After `_lockScroll();`, add `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus({ preventScroll: true });`

issuesFound.title: Inline form validator does not clear aria‑invalid on success
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, inlineValidator removes visual error styling but does not remove aria‑invalid, leaving the invalid state lingering for assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: In _renderResult when result.valid is true add field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid').

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion missing aria‑controls on triggers for dynamically rendered panels
issuesFound.explanation: While the FAQ parser adds aria-controls to each trigger, some FAQ items are generated after the initial load (e.g., via lazy‑load) and their panels lack matching id attributes, breaking the reference.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure every dynamically inserted FAQ panel receives a unique id and the corresponding trigger’s aria-controls is updated accordingly.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop onClick action missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The _onClick() function in the scrollToTop module scrolls the page to the top but does not announce this action to screen readers. Users may lose context of their location on the page without an auditory confirmation that the scroll occurred.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2153: function _onClick() { ... liveRegion.announce('Scrolled back to top of page'); ... }

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent accept and decline actions missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The _onAccept() and _onDecline() functions in the cookieConsent module hide the banner and update consent state but do not announce the result to screen readers. Users are not informed whether their preference was successfully saved.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4353: function _onAccept() { ... liveRegion.announce('Cookie preferences accepted'); ... }
  Line 4363: function _onDecline() { ... liveRegion.announce('Cookie preferences declined'); ... }

It does not set aria-selected="true" on the active tab or aria-selected="false" on inactive tabs, preventing screen readers from identifying which filter is active.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel sets aria-hidden="false" instead of removing attribute

The aria-hidden attribute should be removed entirely (removeAttribute) for visible content; setting it to "false" is unnecessary and can cause compatibility issues with some assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Sticky header hide/show does not announce state to AT
issuesFound.explanation: The `stickyHeader` module adds/removes CSS classes to hide the header on scroll, but does not provide any live‑region announcement (e.g., “Header hidden” / “Header shown”). Users relying on screen readers receive no feedback about the header’s visibility change.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid attribute

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger lacks `aria-controls` linking to its panel (when markup does not provide it)

Ensure the generated `panelId` is applied to the panel element (`panel.id = panelId;`) before setting `aria-controls` on the trigger.
