issuesFound.title: productFilter results not announced
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering products, the visible count is updated in the DOM but not announced to screen readers, so users do not know how many results match their selection or that the filter has been applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line (after _updateCountBadge(totalMatches); in the filterAnimation.run callback): liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid reset
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is cleared or validated successfully, the function removes the error description but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute, leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in markValid, after the if/else block for aria-describedby): removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch initial aria-expanded missing
issuesFound.explanation: When the search combobox is initialized, the aria-expanded attribute is not set to false, violating the requirement that the state be present on page load for collapsed widgets.
issuesFound.fix: Line (after setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox'); in mount function): setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button does not have an aria-controls attribute pointing to the drawer element it controls, preventing screen readers from identifying the relationship between the trigger and the drawer.
issuesFound.fix: Line (after _drawer = qs(SELECTORS.navDrawer || SELECTORS.primaryNav);): if (_trigger && _drawer) { setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav')); }

issuesFound.title: Inline validator never sets aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator._renderResult` adds visual error classes but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on fields that fail validation, so screen readers cannot detect the error state.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close does not reset aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `close()` function hides the mobile navigation drawer but never sets `aria-expanded="false"` on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). After the menu is closed screen‑reader users still hear the button as expanded.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The `open(item, …)` function expands a FAQ panel but never updates the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` to `"true"`. The button remains announced as collapsed even when its panel is visible.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers never update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu`, the click and keydown handlers (`_onTriggerClick`, `_onTriggerKeydown`) toggle the panel visibility but never set `aria-expanded` on the trigger button, leaving the expanded state undisclosed to AT.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid when a field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: When a previously invalid field becomes valid, the code removes visual error styling but leaves `aria-invalid="true"` on the element, causing AT to still announce it as invalid.
issuesFound.fix:
(see fix for Issue 6 – the `removeAttr` call already clears the attribute when the field is valid).  

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle does not announce price changes to AT
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is changed, price values are updated in the UI but no live‑region announcement is made, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the new pricing.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers lack initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdowns are registered, the trigger button is given `aria-haspopup` but no `aria-expanded` attribute is set to reflect the initial collapsed state.

issuesFound.title: Product filter count badge updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: `_updateCountBadge` changes the text of the count badge but the element has no `aria-live` attribute, so users of assistive technology are not informed when the number of matching products changes.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1190: field.setAttribute(ARIA.INVALID, 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions toggle the navigation drawer visibility but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen reader users will not be informed whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1345: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);
Line 1365: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. The close function sets "false", creating an inconsistent state where the open state is never announced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2370: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance functions show/hide the panel but fail to set aria-expanded on the trigger element. Users are not notified of the state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1475: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);
Line 1490: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: productFilter count update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates the product count badge text and aria-label, but does not announce the change to a live region. Users are not informed that the result count has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1750: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter._syncTabAriaStates function updates the visual active state of filter tabs but does not update aria-pressed (or aria-selected). The selected filter is not communicated to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1800: setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on the trigger. The initial state is ambiguous to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1530: setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips render not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The activeFilterChips.render function updates the list of active filter chips dynamically but does not announce the change to a live region. Users are not notified when filters are added or removed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1860: liveRegion.announce('Filters updated');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid function removes the error description but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute. The field may remain stuck in an invalid state programmatically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1205: field.removeAttribute(ARIA.INVALID);

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch.init function configures the input with aria-haspopup but does not set the initial aria-expanded="false". The widget's initial state is undefined.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2285: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function identifies the trigger (_trigger) and drawer (_drawer) but does not link them using aria-controls. Assistive technology cannot associate the button with the panel it controls.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1415: if (_drawer && !_drawer.id) _drawer.id = srUtils.generateId('mobile-nav');
if (_trigger && _drawer) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never activated
issuesFound.explanation: Although a _trap variable is declared, the focus trap is never initialized or activated when the drawer opens. Keyboard focus is not confined to the drawer, allowing users to tab behind the modal overlay.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in open function, after _lockScroll();): if (!_trap) { _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); } _trap.activate();

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu initial aria-expanded missing
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown trigger is registered, the aria-expanded attribute is not initialized to false, even though the panel is hidden by default.
issuesFound.fix: Line (after panel.setAttribute('hidden', ''); in register function): setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Product filter UI changes (count badge, empty‑state) are not announced via a live region (additional to Issue 8)
issuesFound.explanation: Showing or hiding the empty‑state message is not communicated to AT, so users may not know that no products match the current filters.

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard navigation does not set aria‑expanded on step panels (they use `role="tabpanel"` but never update `aria-expanded`).
issuesFound.explanation: When moving between steps, the previously active panel remains with `aria-expanded` unchanged, which can confuse AT about which step is currently visible.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels are hidden with a CSS class but never receive `aria-hidden="true"` when closed (only `hidden` attribute is used after transition). Some AT may not process the delayed `hidden` removal, causing a brief period where the panel is still perceivable.
issuesFound.explanation: The panel is hidden by removing the `open` class and then, after a timeout, setting `hidden`. During the timeout the panel is still focusable.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch._open and _close functions show/hide the suggestion listbox but do not update aria-expanded on the search input. Screen readers will not know when suggestions are available.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2230: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);
Line 2240: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

Product filter UI changes (count badge, empty‑state) are not announced via a live region (additional to Issue 8)

Dropdown menu panels are hidden with a CSS class but never receive `aria-hidden="true"` when closed (only `hidden` attribute is used after transition). Some AT may not process the delayed `hidden` removal, causing a brief period where the panel is still perceivable.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The function associates an error message with a field via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true", so screen readers do not announce the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3402: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after the null check

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open function adds CSS classes to show the navigation drawer but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state change to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2151: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function filters product cards and updates the count badge but does not announce the results (e.g., "Showing 5 products") to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2728: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory) : \`Showing \${totalMatches} products\`);` after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The close function removes CSS classes to hide the drawer but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving screen readers with stale state information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2166: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after the line `_open = false;`

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function switches billing periods and updates prices visually but does not announce the change (e.g., "Prices switched to annual billing") to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2615: Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);` after updating the plan cards

issuesFound.title: pricing._syncToggleState missing aria-pressed update
issuesFound.explanation: The function toggles active CSS classes on billing period buttons but does not update aria-pressed to reflect which button is currently pressed/selected in the toggle button group.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2639: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates missing aria-pressed update
issuesFound.explanation: The function updates visual active states on filter tab buttons but does not set aria-pressed to indicate the active tab to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2789: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function reveals the dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, violating the disclosure pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2283: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function hides the dropdown panel but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button to indicate the collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2296: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._close missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function closes the suggestion listbox but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the combobox input element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2492: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `_searchState.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown toggle button does not update aria‑expanded (open)
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu’s open function (`_openInstance`) adds the “open” class and removes `hidden` from the panel, but never sets `aria‑expanded="true"` on the trigger button. Screen‑reader users cannot know the menu’s state.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance(inst)` add `inst.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded','true');` after opening the panel.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown toggle button does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing a dropdown does not write to any live‑region, so users of assistive technology receive no audible cue that the menu opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: After setting `aria-expanded`, also update a dedicated live‑region (e.g., `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened')` / `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed')`).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown toggle button does not update aria‑expanded (close)
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu’s close function (`_closeInstance`) hides the panel but never sets `aria‑expanded="false"` on the trigger button, leaving the ARIA state stale.
issuesFound.fix: In `_closeInstance(inst,…)` add `inst.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded','false');` before hiding the panel.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestion list is shown/hidden and its content changes, but the only status element (`_statusEl`) is used for error messages, not for announcing the number of suggestions. Users receive no feedback about result count.
issuesFound.fix: After rendering suggestions, set `_statusEl.textContent = `${n} suggestion${n===1?'':'s'} available`;` and ensure `_statusEl` has `aria-live="polite"` (already present).

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The period toggle buttons (`_periodBtns`) receive visual “active” styling but never set `aria‑pressed` to reflect the selected state, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the current billing period.
issuesFound.fix: In `_syncToggleState(period)` after toggling the CSS class, also call `setAttr(btn,'aria-pressed',String(isActive));` for each button.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes (`setPeriod`), the price numbers update but no live‑region announcement is made, so assistive‑technology users may miss the price change.
issuesFound.fix: After updating all price displays, call `liveRegion.announce('Pricing switched to '+BILLING_PERIODS[period].label+' billing');`.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search input does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When the suggestion panel is opened (`_open()`), the script toggles `_searchState.open` but never sets `aria‑expanded="true"` on the search `<input>`. When closed, `aria‑expanded` is never set to `"false"`.
issuesFound.fix: In `_open()` call `setAttr(_inputEl,'aria-expanded','true');` and in `_close()` call `setAttr(_inputEl,'aria-expanded','false');`.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter result count not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the visible product count is updated in the badge (`_updateCount`) but no ARIA live region is used, so users are not informed of how many results remain.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a hidden element with `aria-live="polite"` (e.g., `
`) and set its text to `${count} product${count===1?'':'s'} found` after each filter.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden state not reflected in `aria-hidden`
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is opened, the drawer element receives the “open” class but never has `aria-hidden="false"` removed, nor is `aria-hidden="true"` added when closed. Assistive technologies may still treat it as hidden.
issuesFound.fix: In `open()` call `_drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');` and in `close()` call `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden','true');`.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator aria-invalid not set on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils markInvalid() and markValid() functions manage aria-describedby but do not toggle aria-invalid on the field. Form validation errors are not properly exposed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 895: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');
  Line 905: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger aria-expanded not updated on toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module's open() and close() functions toggle the navigation drawer visibility but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (.nav-toggle). Screen reader users are not informed when the menu state changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1385: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1405: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open() and close() functions emit custom events but do not write a status message to an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not verbally notified when the navigation menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1380: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);
  Line 1400: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger aria-expanded not initialised at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav init() function does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute on the trigger button. The trigger's state is undefined to assistive technologies until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1435: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions do not announce the state change to an aria-live region. Users are not informed when a submenu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1595: liveRegion.announce('Submenu opened');
  Line 1610: liveRegion.announce('Submenu closed');

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons aria-pressed not initialised
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing init() function does not set the initial aria-pressed state on the billing period buttons. The selected state is not communicated to assistive technologies on load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3395: setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing setPeriod() function updates the UI and emits a custom event but does not announce the price change or billing period switch to an aria-live region. Users are not verbally informed of the update.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3315: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual); // Or dynamic message

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger aria-expanded not initialised at registration
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu register() function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute on the trigger. The initial state is missing for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1665: setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion panel aria-hidden not synced with visibility
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion open() and close() functions toggle the hidden attribute on the panel but do not sync aria-hidden. This risks the panel remaining accessible to screen readers when visually collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2705: setAttr(item.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'false');
  Line 2725: setAttr(item.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panel aria-hidden not synced with visibility
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module toggles the hidden attribute on the panel but does not sync aria-hidden. If CSS visibility is used alongside hidden, the panel may remain in the accessibility tree when visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1595: setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'false');
  Line 1610: setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch suggestion list aria-hidden not synced
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestionListRenderer show() and hide() functions toggle the hidden attribute on the listbox but do not sync aria-hidden. The suggestion list may remain in the accessibility tree when visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2335: setAttr(_listEl, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'false');
  Line 2345: setAttr(_listEl, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function expands the FAQ answer panel and adds CSS classes but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, preventing screen readers from announcing the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3155: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function collapses the FAQ answer panel but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving the accessibility tree in an incorrect state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3172: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `item.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The function removes error associations but does not explicitly set aria-invalid="false" to clear the invalid state from assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3412: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` immediately after the null check

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The init function sets up the mobile navigation but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button when the menu is closed by default on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2210: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.init missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The init function registers dropdown triggers but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" on each trigger button when the panels are hidden on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2330: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside the register function or in the init loop after registering each dropdown

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens the suggestion listbox but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the combobox input element to indicate the popup is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2483: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_searchState.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch.init missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The init function sets up the combobox pattern but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" on the search input when the suggestions are hidden on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2535: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after setting up the input element

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens an FAQ item but does not announce the expanded state or question text to screen readers unless the user manually focuses the content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3160: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question));` after opening the panel

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function closes an FAQ item but does not announce the collapsed state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3180: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question));` after closing the panel

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField._uploadEntry missing progress announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function updates a progress bar visually during file upload but does not announce upload progress or completion status to screen readers via the live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4135: Add `liveRegion.announce(\`\${entry.file.name} uploaded successfully\`);` inside the xhr load handler after `entry.status = 'done';` and before resolving

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The period toggle buttons act as a disclosure control for the pricing cards but never set `aria‑expanded` to indicate whether the associated panel (price display) is shown. This violates the ARIA pattern for toggle buttons.
issuesFound.fix: In `_syncToggleState(period)` add `setAttr(btn,'aria-expanded',String(isActive));` for each button.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter applyFilters does not announce filter changes
issuesFound.explanation: The filter operation changes the visible product set, but no live‑region message is emitted, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the new results.
issuesFound.fix: At the end of `applyFilters()`, after updating the count, call `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches===1?'':'s'} displayed`);`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer open/close does not announce state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle (`open()` / `close()`) updates ARIA attributes on the trigger (`aria-expanded`) but does not provide a live‑region announcement, so users may not know when the menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix: After calling `setExpanded(_trigger, true, _drawer);` add `liveRegion.announce('Navigation menu opened');` and similarly announce “closed” on close.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function aria-expanded not updated
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion open() function removes the hidden attribute from the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2705: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs aria-pressed not updated
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter _syncTabAriaStates() function toggles the CSS active class on filter buttons ([data-filter-value]) but does not update aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot identify the active filter.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3975: setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs aria-pressed not initialised
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter init() function calls _syncTabAriaStates() which only toggles classes. The initial aria-pressed state is never set on the filter buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4045: setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: productFilter apply action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter applyFilters() function updates the grid and emits a custom event but does not announce the result count or filter application to an aria-live region. Users are not informed of the content change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3895: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop _onClick() function scrolls the page and moves focus but does not announce the action to an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not verbally confirmed that the page has scrolled to the top.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1835: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.scrolledToTop);

issuesFound.title: Contact form name field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The name input references an error message element via aria-describedby="contact-name-error" when invalid, but no element with this ID exists in the document. This breaks the programmatic association between the field and its error message.
issuesFound.fix: Remove aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? 'contact-name-error' : undefined} from the TextInput, or add <ErrorMessage id="contact-name-error" message={name.state.error} /> inside the FormGroup.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The disclosure button toggles the visibility of the FAQ answer panel using the isOpen prop, but lacks the aria-expanded attribute. Assistive technology users cannot perceive whether individual FAQ items are open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: <button type="button" id={btnId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-controls={panelId} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>

issuesFound.title: Billing toggle buttons missing aria-pressed (annual)
issuesFound.explanation: The annual billing button in the toggle group lacks aria-pressed, failing to indicate its selected state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: <button type="button" id="billing-annual" aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references "newsletter-email-error" via aria-describedby when validation fails, but no element with this ID exists, preventing screen readers from announcing field errors.
issuesFound.fix: Remove aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined} from the TextInput, or add <ErrorMessage id="newsletter-email-error" message={email.state.error} />.

issuesFound.title: Disclosure toggle missing aria-expanded on NavDropdown trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The button that toggles the navigation dropdown does not have an aria-expanded attribute, so screen‑reader users cannot know whether the submenu is open or closed. This affects users of assistive technology navigating the primary navigation.

issuesFound.title: Required field marker (*) not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk used to indicate required fields is announced by screen readers because it lacks aria‑hidden, causing unnecessary verbosity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1310: *

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria-pressed to convey selection
issuesFound.explanation: Each filter‑tab button reflects the active filter via visual styling and tabIndex, but does not expose its pressed state to assistive technology. AT users cannot know which filter is currently applied. This degrades the usability of the product catalogue filter.

issuesFound.title: Loading button in NewsletterForm missing aria-busy
issuesFound.explanation: The submit button uses a custom “loading” prop (loading={isSubmitting}) that the Button component does not recognize, so the button never receives aria‑busy. Assistive technology therefore cannot know the form is submitting, which can cause confusion for users waiting for feedback.

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid propagation
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop but fails to pass aria-invalid to the underlying <input> element. This prevents assistive technology from announcing the error state to users, even when visual styles indicate an error. This affects all forms using this component (NewsletterForm, ContactForm).
issuesFound.fix: Line 536: <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel slide missing aria-hidden when inactive
issuesFound.explanation: Slides that are not currently visible (isVisible is false) remain in the accessibility tree without aria-hidden. Screen readers will announce hidden slides, causing confusion about the current content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1575: <div key={t.id} role="group" aria-roledescription="slide" aria-label={`Quote ${i + 1} of ${total}`} aria-hidden={!isVisible} className={[ 'testimonial-slide', isVisible ? 'testimonial-slide--visible' : '', ].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} >

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop to style the input but does not map it to the aria-invalid attribute. Screen readers cannot announce the error state to users, leaving them unaware of validation failures.
issuesFound.fix: Line 831-842: export const TextInput: FC<TextInputProps> = ({ id, invalid, className, ...rest }) => ( <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} /> );

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle monthly button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly billing button acts as a toggle for the billing period selection (value state) but lacks aria-pressed to indicate whether it is the active selection.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle annual button missing aria-pressed

issuesFound.title: HeroSection video toggle missing aria-pressed

issuesFound.fix: Line ~980: Add aria-pressed={isPlaying} to the Button: `<Button aria-pressed={isPlaying} aria-label={...} ...>`

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input has aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error" when in error state, but FormGroup is not passed the errorId prop, so the error message element is never rendered in the DOM. This creates a broken ARIA reference.

issuesFound.fix: Line 1075: <button type="button" aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav toggle missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button controls the open/close state of the nav panel but lacks aria-expanded. Screen reader users cannot perceive the state of the navigation menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 835: <button id={toggleId} ref={menuToggleRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-label={isOpen ? 'Close navigation menu' : 'Open navigation menu'} className="mobile-nav-toggle" onClick={isOpen ? close : open}>

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks nav missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <nav> element wrapping the skip links is one of multiple navigation landmarks on the page but lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby. Screen reader users navigating by landmarks cannot distinguish this from the main or footer navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 535: <nav aria-label="Skip navigation">

issuesFound.title: HeroSection section missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <section> element for the hero area lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby. While it contains an h1, the landmark itself is not named, making it difficult to identify in landmark navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 905: <section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link for the current page uses a visual class (nav-link--current) based on item.current but lacks the aria-current="page" attribute. Assistive technology cannot identify this as the active page link.
issuesFound.fix: Line 665: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay div missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay <div> has an onClick handler to close the modal but lacks role="button", tabIndex, and onKeyDown. Keyboard users cannot activate this element to close the dialog.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1465: <div role="button" tabIndex={0} className="modal-overlay" aria-modal="true" onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); onClose(); } }}>

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion trigger button controls the visibility of the answer panel but lacks aria-expanded. Assistive technology users cannot know if the answer is currently visible or hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1395: <button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation link for the current page relies on a visual class but omits aria-current="page". This prevents screen readers from announcing the link as the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 755: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: StatsBar section missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <section> containing statistics has an internal sr-only heading but the section element itself lacks aria-labelledby. Landmark navigation will list this section as "unnamed".
issuesFound.fix: Line 995: <section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton for the next slide contains only a visual character ('›') and lacks an aria-label. The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop which is missing here.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1300: <IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons control the activeFilter state to show/hide product categories but do not communicate their pressed state via aria-pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1194: Add aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} to the button: `<button type="button" aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} className="..." ...>`

issuesFound.title: HeroSection section missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section lacks an accessible name despite containing a heading (h1#hero-heading) that could label it.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~956: Add aria-labelledby="hero-heading" to the section: `<section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">`

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing card article element lacks aria-labelledby to reference its heading (h3#headingId), making it difficult for screen reader users to identify which plan the card represents when navigating by region.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1280: Add aria-labelledby={headingId} to the article: `<article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel hidden slides missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Carousel slides that are not visible (isVisible === false) remain in the accessibility tree and are announced by screen readers. They should be hidden with aria-hidden when not visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1360: Add aria-hidden={!isVisible} to the slide div: `<div aria-hidden={!isVisible} role="group" ... className={...}>`

issuesFound.title: Modal dialog aria-modal on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the overlay div instead of the dialog element itself, which may cause screen readers to incorrectly identify the modal boundary.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1520: Remove aria-modal="true" from the overlay. Line ~1523: Add aria-modal="true" to the dialog div: `<div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} ...>`

issuesFound.title: StatItem value not accessible
issuesFound.explanation: The statistic value uses visual formatting (e.g., "40,000+") but does not expose the screen-reader-friendly version (valueLabel) to assistive technology, potentially causing confusing announcements.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~1008: Add aria-label using the valueLabel: `

{stat.value}

`

issuesFound.title: Text inputs missing aria‑invalid when in error state (Newsletter email)
issuesFound.explanation: The `<TextInput>` receives an invalid prop but does not forward it to aria‑invalid, so AT cannot detect the error.
issuesFound.fix:
Modify TextInput component to spread `aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined}` or add the attribute where the component is used. Example fix at usage:  
  Line 822: `<TextInput`  
  Line 823: `  id="newsletter-email"`  
  Line 824: `  type="email"`  
  Line 825: `  ...`  
  Line 826: `  aria-invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error}` ← add this attribute

issuesFound.explanation: The input’s aria-describedby points to `newsletter-email-error`, but no element with that ID is rendered, causing a broken reference.
issuesFound.fix:
Either render an error element with that ID or remove the aria-describedby when there is no error. Example fix – change the attribute to reference the actual ErrorMessage ID or omit when no error:  
  Line 822: `aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined}` → `aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email' : undefined}` (and ensure an element with that ID exists).

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop to style the field in error state but does not map this to aria-invalid="true". Screen reader users cannot programmatically determine that the field contains invalid data.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1012-1023: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} to the input element: <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} ...>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav accordion trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion trigger button in MobileNav uses aria-controls to reference the submenu but does not have aria-expanded to indicate whether the section is expanded or collapsed. This prevents screen reader users from understanding the current state of the navigation sections.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1134-1142: Add aria-expanded={isExpanded} to the button element: <button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} ...>

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown decorative chevron not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron indicator (▲/▼) inside the NavDropdown trigger is decorative text content that is not hidden from screen readers. Screen readers may announce the triangle characters as "black up-pointing triangle" or similar, creating noise.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1042-1044: Add aria-hidden="true" to the span: {isOpen ? '▲' : '▼'}

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required field marker not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The required field marker asterisk (*) in FormGroup is not hidden from assistive technology with aria-hidden="true". Screen readers may announce "star" or "asterisk" separately from the "(required)" text that follows, creating redundant verbosity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1080: Add aria-hidden="true" to the span: *

issuesFound.title: SiteHeader mobile toggle missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button in SiteHeader has aria-controls but lacks aria-expanded to indicate whether the mobile navigation panel is currently open or closed. This prevents assistive technology from announcing the state change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1242-1252: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button element: <button id={toggleId} ref={menuToggleRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} ...>

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks component renders a <nav> element without an aria-label. Since there are multiple navigation landmarks on the page (main nav, footer nav, etc.), screen reader users cannot distinguish the purpose of this skip link navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 952: Add aria-label="Skip links" to the nav element: <nav aria-label="Skip links">

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop for styling but does not map it to aria-invalid. Screen reader users cannot detect when the textarea contains invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1026-1036: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} to the textarea element: <textarea id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} ...>

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The ThemeToggle component renders an IconButton with only an emoji icon (☀️/🌙/💻) as content and no aria-label prop. The IconButton interface requires an aria-label, but it is not provided, leaving screen reader users without a description of the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1163-1168: Add aria-label prop to IconButton: <IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">{ICONS[theme]}</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link in DesktopNav that represents the current page (item.current === true) does not have aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot identify which page is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1143-1148: Add aria-current={item.current ? "page" : undefined} to the anchor: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? "page" : undefined} ...>

issuesFound.title: HeroSection region missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The HeroSection component uses a <section> element without aria-labelledby or aria-label. The section contains a heading with id="hero-heading", but the section does not reference it, leaving the landmark unnamed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1273-1275: Add aria-labelledby="hero-heading" to the section: <section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The previous slide button in TestimonialsCarousel is an IconButton containing only the "‹" character with no aria-label. The IconButton interface requires an aria-label prop, but it is missing, so screen reader users hear only "less than" or silence.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1762-1768: Add aria-label="Previous testimonial" (or similar) to the IconButton: <IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} ...>

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The next slide button in TestimonialsCarousel is an IconButton containing only the "›" character with no aria-label. The IconButton interface requires an aria-label prop, but it is missing, so screen reader users hear only "greater than" or silence.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1769-1775: Add aria-label="Next testimonial" (or similar) to the IconButton: <IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} ...>

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading state
